methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $87M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Osaro, a Series B robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Derik Pridmore and Michael Kahane, has raised $86.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector companies at Series B or Series C stage, supplemented by a broader comparable universe of 1 public company and 8 private peers including Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $87M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage transitions, yielding $165M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the median valuation across the 4 closest comparables—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Hadrian ($1.6B), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B)—producing a $1.6B implied valuation, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B.

The two methods diverge materially, reflecting the tension between company-specific capital efficiency and peer-set benchmarking. The triangulated range is $140M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $895M. Key uncertainties include whether Osaro's technology maturity and revenue trajectory align with higher-valuation peers like Bedrock, and whether the stage-progression method—relying on fundraising as a proxy—captures sufficient growth optionality. The wide range suggests further diligence on unit economics, customer concentration, and technical differentiation is warranted to narrow the valuation band.
